completed_steps.summary: Classified as an npm/Turborepo monorepo with five logical parts: API, web, mobile, database, and shared packages.
Inventoried 34 existing project, application, package, test, operations, planning, domain, and process documents; user requested even coverage.
Documented the current TypeScript stack and architecture patterns for all five parts using manifests, lockfile, configuration, and critical source files.
Completed conditional deep analysis and wrote API contracts, database models, web/mobile component inventories, and shared-package documentation.
Generated an annotated current-state source tree covering all five logical parts and cross-cutting test, delivery, infrastructure, and BMAD directories.
Generated current-state development, deployment, and contribution guides from manifests, scripts, CI, environment handling, and repository policy.
Documented cross-part HTTP, contract, identity, database, worker, realtime, notification, analytics, observability, and deployment integrations.
Generated current-state architecture documents for API, web, mobile, database, and shared-package parts.
Completed supporting documentation with the project overview and machine-readable five-part integration metadata; all expected deep-scan documents exist.
Generated the master AI retrieval index with five-part quick reference, all generated documents, curated existing knowledge, and getting-started guidance.
Validated all generated Markdown and JSON, found no incomplete markers, and received user approval to finalize.
Completed the deep brownfield documentation workflow with index.md as the primary retrieval entry point.

project_classification.summary: NestJS API, Next.js web app, Expo mobile app, Prisma data layer, and shared TypeScript libraries.

technology_stack.api: Node.js 24, TypeScript 5.9, NestJS 11.1, Prisma 6.19, BullMQ 5, Socket.io 4
technology_stack.mobile: Expo 54, Expo Router 6, React Native 0.81, React 19.1
technology_stack.database: PostgreSQL/Supabase with Prisma 6.19 and SQL RLS migrations
technology_stack.shared: Zod/OpenAPI generated SDK, shared configuration, utilities, testing factories, k6 helpers, and ESLint

architecture_patterns.api: Modular feature-first NestJS with controller-service-repository layers and separate worker bootstrap
architecture_patterns.web: Next.js App Router with server route shells and client interactive leaves
architecture_patterns.mobile: Expo Router with route wrappers and feature screens using local state
architecture_patterns.database: Prisma schema plus ordered SQL migrations as the RLS/security source of truth
architecture_patterns.shared: Workspace libraries with explicit exports and a Zod-to-OpenAPI-to-generated-SDK contract pipeline

batches_completed.summary: Controllers, contracts, modules, workers, security, configuration, and tests analyzed.
Routes, components, data access, analytics, configuration, and tests analyzed.
Expo routes, feature screens, API integration, analytics, notifications, assets, and tests analyzed.
Prisma schema, migrations, seeds, RLS, constraints, and database tests analyzed.
Shared contracts, SDK generation, configuration, utilities, testing, k6, and lint packages analyzed.

verification.summary: Prettier passed for all 17 generated Markdown documents; both JSON files parsed; index links were validated; git diff whitespace checks passed.
verification.open_risks: Current implementation risks are documented, including missing client authentication, no worker deployment target, unauthenticated admin routes, stale planning versions, and production test-safety gaps.
verification.next_checks: Review and prioritize documented current-state risks before production deployment; run code validation separately because this workflow changed documentation and configuration only.

resume_instructions: Workflow complete. Start from index.md for AI-assisted brownfield development.
